jobs.title: Case Manager (Community Supports Navigator)
jobs.description:
Description




Position Summary: Community Supports Navigators(CSN) will assist individuals and families who are experiencing homelessness or are at risk of homelessness. The CSN will be responsible for outreach and engagement, helping individuals connect with the necessary resources and services to secure and maintain stable housing. Additionally, the CSN will assist Members in acquiring, retaining, and improving self-help, socialization, and adaptive skills necessary to reside successfully in the person's natural environment. 




City Net's programs always follow Housing First and Harm Reduction service models. 




City Net's programs and services are housing focused. 




Job Duties & Responsibilities 





 Conduct outreach to individuals and families experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness in various locations including shelters, street outreach, community centers, and other public spaces.
 Build relationships with community organizations, landlords, and property managers to identify and secure housing opportunities for clients.
 Provide assessment and support to clients to identify their needs, strengths, and barriers to obtaining and maintaining stable housing.
 Assist clients in accessing and applying for affordable housing programs, rental subsidies, and other financial assistance as appropriate.
 Connect clients with social service agencies, healthcare providers, and other resources to address any physical or mental health issues, financial concerns, or other barriers to housing stability.
 Work collaboratively with other service providers that client is connected to ensure optimal service coordination.
 Facilitate and coordinate communication between clients and landlords, property managers, and other service providers to ensure a smooth transition into permanent housing.
 Develop and maintain client files and documentation according to agency and program guidelines including entry into HMIS and other databases.
 Participate in regular training and professional development opportunities to stay up to date on housing policies and best practices.
 Attend community meetings and events related to homelessness and housing to represent the agency and advocate for clients.
 Provide culturally appropriate health education information and outreach in community-based settings, such as homes, clinics, shelters, local businesses, and community centers for various Managed Care Plan (MCP) members.
 Transports clients in City Net vehicle as needed.




Requirements




Job Skills and Requirements 





 A passion for helping vulnerable populations reach their potential, with a desire to make a difference.
 Bachelor's or Master's degree in social work, public administration, or a related field.
 Knowledge and understanding of regulations related to housing development and implementation of housing programs.
 Experience in providing training and consultation to staff and external stakeholders.
 Familiarity with the CalAIM program and its requirements.
 Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
 Ability to work independently and collaboratively with diverse stakeholders.
 Proficiency in using HMIS software and other relevant technology tools.
 Excellent organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
 Strong interpersonal skills and ability to establish rapport with service providers and other stakeholders.
 Ability to travel locally to attend meetings, trainings, and conferences as needed.
 For insurance purposes, needs to be at least 21 years old, must have a valid drivers' license, and be approved to drive a City Net vehicle by our insurance company. Employees driving their own personal vehicle for work purposes will also need to show valid liability insurance.
 Displays patience and self-control with employees, clients, customers, and the public-at-large.
 Working knowledge of the Microsoft Windows OS, Microsoft Office 365, and the Internet.

jobs.title: New Patient Care Coordinator - Sacramento Natural Dentistry
jobs.description:
New Patient Care Coordinator

Location: Citrus Heights, CA

Job Type: Full-time (Monday – Friday)

Compensation: $18.00 – $23.00 per hour (Depending on Experience)

Are you an experienced dental professional with top-notch customer service skills looking to take your career to the next level? If so, we want to meet you!

At Sacramento Natural Dentistry, we are a close-knit, passionate group of dental professionals looking to add a talented New Patient Care Coordinator to our practice. We specialize in holistic and biological care, delivering high-quality, patient-centered health solutions in a fast-paced and team-oriented environment.

Why Choose Us?



A Stellar Team Culture: Work with an organized, energetic, and supportive team that values daily positive feedback.Growth Opportunities: Expand your knowledge with excellent continuing education opportunities.Work-Life Balance: Enjoy standard Monday through Friday hours-no weekends!Competitive Benefits:Paid Time Off (PTO)Paid Holidays (always a major plus!)Medical BenefitsRole Responsibilities

As Our New Patient Care Coordinator, You Will Be The Welcoming Face And Voice Of Our Practice, Helping New Patients Seamlessly Transition Into Our Care. Your Daily Impact Will Include

Acting as the primary point of contact for onboarding new patients, ensuring a warm and informative introduction to our holistic dental philosophy.Managing new patient intake, scheduling appointments efficiently, and managing follow-ups.Answering phone calls and digital inquiries with a high level of professionalism, empathy, and clarity.Coordinating patient check-ins, updating digital health histories, and verifying insurance information.

Qualifications & Requirements

Education: High school diploma or equivalent (Required).Experience: Minimum 2–3 years of dental office experience (Required).Technical Skills: At least 2 years of strong computer skills and familiarity with dental practice management software (Required).Interpersonal Skills: Outstanding communication, a positive attitude, and a genuine passion for patient care.

Apply Today!

Not to toot our own horn, but given how wonderful and stable our current staff is, it's rare that we have an opening on our team. We don't expect this opportunity to stay available for long!

If you are ready to join a practice that values your skills and treats you like family, don't let this opportunity fly by-apply today! We are incredibly excited to hear from you.

jobs.title: Housing Navigator: ERAP & Rental Counseling
jobs.description: Young Community Developers is hiring a Housing Specialist to manage Emergency Rental Assistance and Housing Counseling services in San Francisco. The role involves client management and case administration to help low-income tenants facing housing instability. Ideal candidates will possess a degree in a related field or equivalent experience and demonstrate strong cross-cultural communication skills. The position offers a supportive work environment dedicated to breaking the cycle of poverty. #J-18808-Ljbffr

jobs.title: Clinical RN II - Care Coordinator & Unit Leader
jobs.description: Palomar Health in Escondido, California is seeking a Registered Nurse to deliver coordinated nursing care and manage clinical activities within a unit. Candidates should have at least 1 year of RN experience in critical care or specialty areas and a current CA RN License. The role includes supervising nursing staff and adhering to required standards and practices. Competitive candidates will be detail-oriented with strong computer skills and the ability to follow established protocols. #J-18808-Ljbffr

jobs.title: Behavioral Health Coordinator
jobs.description:
RCMS is looking for a Behavioral Health Coordinator who brings Medical Assisting skills, a community-centered mindset, and strong organization and communication skills to support our Psychiatric and Counseling services. This role is perfect for someone who cares deeply about people and is also comfortable managing paperwork, coordination, and follow-through. This is a part time 3 day a week position.

What This Role Is Really About

You’ll be a key connector—between patients and providers, between Behavioral Health and the rest of the clinic, and between people and the resources that support their wellbeing.



Greet, room, and support patients coming in for Behavioral Health and Psychiatric appointmentsTake and document vital signs Support Psychiatric and Behavioral Health providers with visit flow, scheduling, and follow-upCoordinate Behavioral Health referrals and help keep access to care movingAssist with medication refills and prior authorizationsFollow up with patients who miss appointments and help reschedule when neededWork closely with clinic managers and front office teams to keep schedules running smoothlyManage a significant amount of documentation and paperwork, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and confidentiality

You Might Be a Great Fit If…

You have Medical Assisting experience and are comfortable taking vitalsYou are highly organized and able to manage multiple tasks, deadlines, and workflowsYou have excellent communication skills and can connect easily with patients, providers, and staffYou care deeply about community health and Behavioral Health accessYou stay calm and focused in a busy clinic environmentYou believe small moments of kindness and clear communication make a big difference 

Nice to Have (But Not Required)

Experience in Behavioral Health, Psychiatry, or Primary CareFamiliarity with electronic health recordsBackground in community health, social services, or an FQHCKnowledge of local community resources 

Why RCMS

At RCMS, we serve a rural coastal community where relationships matter. This role offers the opportunity to support patients in meaningful ways, help providers deliver compassionate care, and be part of a collaborative, mission-driven team.

If you’re looking for work that balances people, purpose, and organization—and want to feel good about what you do each day—we’d love to hear from you.

Apply today and help support Behavioral Health care in our community.

Salary Description $22/hr - $30/hr

jobs.title: Night-Shift LVN Care Coordinator Senior Living
jobs.description: Marbella San Jose is looking for a dedicated LVN to join our team on a Part Time Night shift (10 PM to 6 AM). This role involves developing treatment plans, communicating with patients and families, and implementing nursing practices to ensure quality care. We offer competitive wages, flexible scheduling, and comprehensive benefits for full-time staff. Join us if you want to make a significant impact on the lives of others! #J-18808-Ljbffr

jobs.title: Care Manager
jobs.description:
We are excited to invite passionate individuals to apply for the role of Care Manager at Independent Living Systems (ILS). Our mission is to enhance the quality of life and promote independence for vulnerable populations through our affiliated health plans, Florida Community Care and Florida Complete Care.

About the Role:

The Care Manager plays a crucial role in coordinating and managing comprehensive care plans for individuals requiring health and social support services. You will ensure that members receive personalized, effective, and timely care by collaborating closely with healthcare providers, social workers, and family members. Acting as a liaison among all parties involved, you will advocate for clients' needs and preferences while monitoring progress and adjusting care plans as needed. This role is focused on improving client outcomes, enhancing quality of life, and optimizing resource utilization within the care continuum.

Minimum Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in social work, Psychology, Biology, Public Health, Nursing, Community Health, or a related field, or equivalent experience.Minimum of 5 years of experience working with individuals facing complex health and social challenges.Familiarity with community agencies and programs.Experience with Medi-Cal eligibility guidelines and the application/renewal process.Strong problem-solving and customer service skills.Must be a resident of California and reside there while employed.Valid California Driver's License.Personal vehicle required; current vehicle registration must be provided.Proof of auto insurance with California minimum coverage.BCLS CPR Certification is mandatory.Preferred Qualifications:

Master's degree in Nursing, Social Work, Public Health, or Healthcare Administration.Certified Case Manager (CCM) certification or equivalent.Experience working with diverse populations, including the elderly, disabled, or chronically ill clients.Bilingual abilities, particularly in Spanish or other widely spoken languages in California.Key Responsibilities:

Develop and manage individualized care plans for clients in your caseload, ensuring effective care coordination.Assess the psychosocial and health needs of high-risk members and document your findings in appropriate systems.Consult and refer members to licensed staff as necessary based on their specific needs.Build relationships and collaborate with community resources, health plans, and providers by engaging with local agencies, including faith-based organizations and community centers.Assist members in overcoming barriers related to complex health conditions by connecting them with necessary services, including home visits and appointment accompaniment.Establish trust and rapport with clients and their support systems to promote effective communication.Identify social determinants of health and address gaps through documented plans.Update member status and progress notes accurately in appropriate systems, ensuring timely reporting for improved outcomes.Recognize gaps in community resources and medical systems, recommending and implementing solutions as needed.
